element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Enemies deploy in swirling patterns from center or edges; player learns circular perimeter movement
Three satellites gyrate in small circles; destroying center sun-like satellite grants double guns upgrade
Fly straight outward from center at regular intervals; give point bonus if shot but cannot be destroyed
Two generator segments with beam between; destroying either deactivates beam; lethal on contact
Costs energy; provided alongside standard guns as upgrade option
Emergency defensive tool for overwhelming enemy waves
Resource management for extended play; also awarded at score thresholds

element_introduction_order.first_appears: When reaching each planet (Neptune, Uranus, Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Earth, Venus, Mercury, Sun)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culmination of stage patterns; defeat grants access to bonus stage
Risk-free shooting gallery; enemies cannot destroy player; scoring focus (100 or 10,000 points per ship)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Between Neptune and Uranus in NES version
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Additional stage milestone not in arcade original

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus stage after planet boss (safe shooting gallery)
Enemy waves completing deployment and hovering near center
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy ships firing while circling perimeter
Transitions between 'warps' (2 warps to Neptune, then 3 warps to next planets)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity with rhythmic deployment patterns; enemies appear in coordinated waves with shooting phases interspersed with travel between celestial bodies; bonus stages provide controlled relief valves between danger phases

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Satellites provide variable weapon upgrades; player choice to pursue or avoid based on position
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: One-point perspective with vanishing point at screen center creates depth tunnel effect; shooting always converges toward center

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 and every 10th stage have enemies that do not fire on player when entering, establishing safe learning phases
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of laser beam generators and increased enemy coordination; player gains access to energy-based super phaser and smart bomb pickups
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mercury, Venus, and Sun stages with accumulated enemy types; final 3-warp return to Neptune requires sustained high-speed play
notable_spikes: Transitions between planetary systems requiring faster enemy patterns
Mercury and Venus stages with compressed enemy deployment windows

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as journey through solar system: 2 warps to Neptune, then 3 warps each to Uranus, Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Earth, with bonus stages between bosses serving as tension relief. After Earth, 3-warp accelerated return to Neptune.
Stages 1, 11, 21, 31 feature enemies that do not fire when entering screen, allowing player to learn deployment patterns without immediate threat
Majority of stage features spaceships appearing from center or edges in swirling patterns, hovering near center after deployment, then occasionally firing outward before gradually flying away
After defeating planet boss, player enters safe bonus round where enemy ships cannot destroy player; scoring-focused with 100 or 10,000 point variations for 40 ships
